name: Scope – While Policy
description: Demonstrates the while-scope policy. After scope_close completes the orchestrator evaluates the condition expression against the current session data. If it evaluates to True (and max_iterations has not been reached) all scope tasks are reset to PENDING for another iteration. The condition reads 'elements_remaining', which 'init' seeds as a declared output. Since the sleep tasks standing in for real work never decrement it, this example loops until max_iterations caps it and then moves on to finalise.

tasks.description: Initialisation step before the loop. Seeds the counter the while condition reads – a real agent would compute it from the model instead of declaring a fixed value.

outputs.name: elements_remaining
outputs.description: How many elements are left to process. Declared with a value so the count exists in session data before the first evaluation of the while condition.

tasks.description: Opens the while scope. Condition expression and optional max_iterations are declared in while_policy.
Pick the next element to process. A real agent would declare 'elements_remaining' as an output here too and return the decremented count, which is what ends the loop before max_iterations.
Closes the scope. The orchestrator evaluates while_policy.condition against session data.
Runs once, after all elements have been processed.
